name: GH_UndoOperation
summary: Enumerates all possible types of undo operation.

enum_values.name: ClearUndoStack
enum_values.summary: The stack with all undo records has been cleared.

enum_values.name: ClearRedoStack
enum_values.summary: The stack with all redo records has been cleared. This always happens when a new undo record is added.

enum_values.name: RecordAdded
enum_values.summary: A new undo record is added to the list. There will be a ClearRedoStack event after this one.

enum_values.name: RecordRemoved
enum_values.summary: A specific record has been deleted. This is not a typical operation and is usually due to user intervention. Removal can occur from both the undo and the redo stack.

enum_values.name: Undo
enum_values.summary: An undo operation was successfully completed. This means a record has been moved from the undo stack to the redo stack.

enum_values.name: Redo
enum_values.summary: A redo operation was successfully completed. This means a record has been moved from the redo stack back onto the undo stack. This does not count as a RecordAdded operation.
